clues: Particularly harmful to young children and women of child-bearing age, exposure to this causes over 1.5 million deaths annually primarily due to cardiovascular effects
Although typically measured in the blood, this can mimic calcium and is stored in teeth and bones for decades
Often linked to old paint, exposure can cause hypertension, anemia, and damage to the brain
Discovered in the late 1980s, this medication was found on the top of Mount Ararat in Turkey
Due to musculoskeletal side effects, this medication was tested on dogs prior to approval for human use. This side effect is also why you still check CK levels with use.
This antibiotic is ineffective against pneumonia since it is inactivated by pulmonary surfactant
In addition to human use, this medication can help plant immunity and growth
Based on the 2018 ARRIVE, ASCEND, and ASPREE trials, guidelines reconsidered our use of this medication in healthy populations
Derived from willow bark, this medication was used in ancient Egyptian and Sumerian civilizations as a pain reliever

Now widely prescribed, these medications were discovered in the early 1970s by a Japanese biochemist who was inspired by Alexander Fleming's penicillin discovery. Akira Endo screened thousands of fungal cultures, discovering a key compound produced by the mold Penicillium.
Among the most commonly prescribed class of medications globally, use of these medications continues to grow as the population ages
Commonly associated with muscle aches, this group of medications is used to reduce the risk of heart attacks and strokes
Due to photosensitizing effects, the FDA has recently updated labels for this medication warning of an increased risk of non-melanoma skin cancer
Approved in 1959 and on the WHO list of essential medications, this drug prevents sodium reabsorption at the distal convoluted tubule
Approved to treat high blood pressure, this medication can cause numerous electrolyte disturbances including hyperuricemia and hypercalcemia
